title: NBA playoffs: Wolves, 76ers win, Jaylen Brown scuffles with Jamal Murray
body:
The 2026 NBA playoffs have been nothing short of thrilling, and Thursday night's action was no exception. In a dramatic turn of events, two teams closed out their first-round series, while another managed to stave off elimination. The New York Knicks and the Minnesota Timberwolves were the big winners of the night, with the Knicks dispatching the Atlanta Hawks and the Wolves pulling off a stunning upset against the Denver Nuggets. Meanwhile, the Philadelphia 76ers kept their postseason hopes alive, setting up a thrilling conclusion to their series.

The Knicks' victory over the Hawks was a testament to their dominance throughout the series. Despite facing a spirited effort from their opponents, the Knicks' depth and talent ultimately proved too much for the Hawks to handle. The win marked a significant milestone for the Knicks, who will now look to carry their momentum into the next round of the playoffs. For the Hawks, the loss brought a disappointing end to a season that had shown promise at times, but ultimately fell short of expectations.

The Wolves' upset victory over the Nuggets, on the other hand, was a major shockwave in the NBA playoff landscape. Despite being undermanned and facing significant adversity, the Wolves managed to dig deep and find a way to win. The series win was a testament to the team's resilience and determination, and marked a major breakthrough for a franchise that has struggled to find consistency in recent years. For the Nuggets, the loss was a bitter pill to swallow, as they had been expected to dominate the series from the outset.

The 76ers' win over the Boston Celtics was another major storyline from Thursday night's action. After falling behind 3-1 in the series, the 76ers have managed to stay alive, setting up a thrilling conclusion to the series. The Celtics, meanwhile, will be looking to regroup and refocus after a disappointing loss. The series has been marked by controversy, including a scuffle between Jaylen Brown and Jamal Murray, and it will be interesting to see how the teams navigate the challenges ahead.

As the playoffs continue to unfold, it's clear that this year's tournament is going to be full of surprises. The Wolves' upset victory over the Nuggets is a prime example of how quickly the landscape can shift, and teams can't afford to take anything for granted. The 76ers' refusal to give up against the Celtics is another testament to the never-say-die attitude that defines the NBA postseason. As the remaining teams look to advance to the next round, they'll need to be prepared for anything.

Looking ahead to the next round of the playoffs, it's clear that the competition is only going to get fiercer. The Knicks and the Wolves will be looking to build on their momentum, while the 76ers will be seeking to complete an improbable comeback against the Celtics. With the stakes higher than ever, the NBA playoffs are shaping up to be an unforgettable ride. As the teams take to the court for the next round of action, one thing is certain: only the strongest will survive. The question on everyone's mind is, who will be the last team standing? Only time will tell, but one thing is for sure - the 2026 NBA playoffs have been an unforgettable journey so far, and there's still plenty more drama to come.
